steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: We need to factor $158t^3 - 24t^2u$ by pulling out the greatest common factor (GCF) from both terms.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$2t(79t^2 - 12tu)$': only factors out $2t$, leaving $t$ still inside both terms — not fully factored. ✗
  • Choice B — '$2t^2(79t - 12u)$': GCF of $158t^3$ and $24t^2u$ is $2t^2$. Factoring gives $2t^2(79t - 12u)$. Check: $2t^2 \cdot 79t = 158t^3$ and $2t^2 \cdot 12u = 24t^2u$. ✓
  • Choice C — '$2t^2(79t + 12u)$': wrong sign on the second term. ✗
  • Choice D — '$2t^3(79 - 12u)$': $2t^3 \cdot 79 = 158t^3$ but $2t^3 \cdot 12u = 24t^3u \ne 24t^2u$. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
